mnemonics: PMF for 'Particular' Mass Function (discrete), PDF for 'Pervasive' Density Function (continuous).
keyPoints: A Probability Mass Function (PMF) gives the probability that a discrete random variable is exactly equal to some value.
A Probability Density Function (PDF) describes the relative likelihood for a continuous random variable to take on a given value; probability is found by integrating the PDF over an interval.

Mathematical Expectation (the expected value) is the long-run average value of repetitions of the experiment it represents; it is a weighted average of all possible values, calculated as Σ [x * P(x)] for discrete variables and ∫ [x * f(x)] dx for continuous variables.
commonMistakes: Using a PMF (summation) for a continuous variable or a PDF (integration) for a discrete variable.
Interpreting the value of a PDF at a single point as a probability, rather than the probability density.
Forgetting that expectation is a theoretical average, not necessarily a value the variable can actually assume.
